title: Free SRS Next Review Calculator | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate when to review flashcards using the SM-2 spaced repetition algorithm. Free SRS calculator for language learners.
intro: Learning a language requires sustained hours of targeted exposure. According to FSI research, Spanish speakers need: Italian/Portuguese ~600h, English/French ~900h, German ~1100h, Arabic/Chinese ~2200h. The key: consistency beats intensity. Use this free SRS calculator to determine when to review each flashcard using the proven SM-2 algorithm. Data updated to 2026 and checked regularly.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **SuperMemo SM-2**. Calculate your next review date by entering your last review interval, E-Factor, and current interval. Get your next SRS review date instantly.
useCases: Teaching and learning the SM-2 algorithm
explanation:
## Spaced Repetition and the SM-2 Algorithm

Language learning is a marathon requiring hundreds of hours. Cognitive science (Ebbinghaus, spaced repetition) combined with immersion and active practice form the evidence-based foundation proven by FSI and Cambridge.

## How It Works

This calculator applies the SuperMemo SM-2 formula with your values, checking for reasonable ranges and alerting you if inputs fall outside typical parameters.

The SM-2 algorithm schedules reviews at increasing intervals. Your E-Factor (ease/difficulty) adjusts after each review based on performance:
- **New interval = Current interval × E-Factor**
- E-Factor ranges typically 1.3 (hard) to 2.5+ (easy)
- Reviewing at the right moment combats the forgetting curve

## Final Notes

This calculator is a planning tool. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a qualified professional. Data updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: SuperMemo's spaced repetition algorithm, used in most modern flashcard apps to optimize review timing.
Ease factor: a measure of how difficult you find a card, typically ranging 1.3–2.5. Higher E-Factors mean longer gaps between reviews.
SM-2 schedules increasingly longer gaps: 1 day, 6 days, then 6×E-Factor days, and so on. Longer gaps reduce review sessions once cards are memorized.
Yes. Anki uses a modified SM-2 algorithm, so this calculator applies to Anki decks.

faq.q: What if I fail a card?
faq.a: The interval resets (you review sooner), but your E-Factor is preserved and adjusted based on performance.
Yes, all Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free and require no account.
Results are guidelines based on internationally validated formulas. For critical decisions, consult a professional.
No. All calculations happen in your browser. We never send data to any server.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data required
